programme.title: Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby questions his lifestyle, as well as the suspects, after a series of deaths take place at a health retreat.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The remains of a racing driver turn up at Darnley Park School, 40 years after he was thought to have drowned in the Lake District.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a social services investigator is found dead in a river, Barnaby has a number of suspects who have all had a run-in with the investigator.

programme.title: Desperation Road
programme.desc: A woman and her daughter are caught in the crossfire when whiskey, guns, and revenge violently intersect.

programme.title: Mad Money
programme.desc: Three female employees of the Federal Reserve plot to steal money that is about to be destroyed.

programme.title: Queenpins
programme.desc: A pair of housewives create a $40 million coupon scam.

programme.title: Life of Crime
programme.desc: Two criminals kidnap the wife of a corrupt developer who is not interested in paying the $1 million ransom.

programme.title: 10th & Wolf [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: While serving in the military, a Marine sergeant (James Marsden) believes his family's ties to organized crime are a thing of the past. However, an act of insubordination and a visit from the F.B.I. send him packing to his South Philadelphia hometown, and he discovers that his brother (Brad Renfro) and his cousin (Giovanni Ribisi) have become entangled with mobsters, leaving him with only one way to save them.

programme.title: The Code
programme.desc: A veteran thief recruits a younger crook to help him pull off one final job in order to repay his debt to the Russian mob.

programme.title: Danger Zone
programme.desc: Rick Morgan (Billy Zane), an American engineer who runs a mine in East Africa, is approached one day by his friend Jim Scott (Robert Downey Jr.), who needs someplace to store canisters of toxic waste. Unfortunately, a spill occurs a short time later, resulting in deaths and Morgan having to leave the country. A year later, he is approached by a government agent (Ron Silver) looking for a missing supply of potentially hazardous poison, which in truth hides something even more valuable.

programme.title: Blitz
programme.desc: Jason Statham stars as a tough cop who's dispatched to take down a serial killer who has been targeting police officers. Also featuring Paddy Considine and Aidan Gillen.

programme.title: The Air I Breathe [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: A drama based on an ancient Chinese proverb that breaks life down into four emotional cornerstones: happiness, pleasure, sorrow and love.

programme.title: The Infiltrator
programme.desc: A U.S. Customs official uncovers a money laundering scheme involving Colombian drug lord Pablo Escobar.

programme.title: Death And The Maiden
programme.desc: Death And The Maiden

programme.title: The Stepfather
programme.desc: A man becomes suspicious of his new stepfather.

programme.title: Bordertown
programme.desc: A journalist uncovers a series of murders.

programme.title: Quicksilver
programme.desc: After losing his life savings playing the market, a trader quits his job and becomes a bicycle messenger.

programme.title: Fatal Contact: Bird Flu In America
programme.desc: When an outbreak of avian flu mutates into a virus transmittable from human to human, scientists race against time to save the world from the worst biological terror it has ever faced.

programme.title: Little Nikita
programme.desc: A timely espionage thriller which stars River Phoenix as a seemingly average teenager, except in one respect--his real name is Nikita. River Phoenix has been living a dangerous lie and Sidney Poitier is the secret agent who forces him to face the hard truth about his parents true identity in this riveting spy thriller.

programme.title: Mortal Thoughts
programme.desc: Two New Jersey hairdressers try to cover up a murder without destroying their friendship.

programme.title: The Game That Kills
programme.desc: Alec Ferguson (Charles Quigley) joins a professional hockey team to find out who was responsible for the faked rink accident that resulted in his brother's death. Rita Hayworth, in one of her first major screen roles, plays the daughter of the team's trainer.

programme.title: The Doctor Takes A Wife
programme.desc: A publicity stunt links a fiercely independent writer in matrimony with a chauvinistic medical school professor.

programme.title: Born Free
programme.desc: The true story of Joy Adamson and her husband, George, who adopt a slain lioness' cubs.

programme.title: The Fuller Brush Girl
programme.desc: A scatterbrained door-to-door cosmetics saleswoman and her bumbling file clerk fiancé become prime suspects in a murder.

programme.title: Gidget Grows Up
programme.desc: Gidget invades the U.N. as a tour guide after her boyfriend, now in the Air Force, goes off without calling her. After many hectic schemes to help world peace and many twists and turns, Gidget's love life is settled.

programme.title: Bloodfist III: Forced to Fight
programme.desc: Don "The Dragon" Wilson is caught between two other races at war in prison.

programme.title: Coffee And Cigarettes
programme.desc: A series of vignettes that all have coffee and cigarettes in common.

programme.title: Plan 9 From Outer Space
programme.desc: Evil aliens attack Earth and set their terrible "Plan 9" into action. As the aliens resurrect the dead of the Earth, the lives of the living are in danger.

programme.title: Before The Devil Knows You're Dead
programme.desc: Master filmmaker Sidney Lumet (The Verdict, Dog Day Afternoon, Serpico) delivers "one of his greatest achievements" (Roger Ebert) with this riveting and compelling suspense thriller. Oscar-winner* Philip Seymour Hoffman (Capote) is Andy, an overextended payroll executive who lures his younger brother, Hank (Ethan Hawke, Training Day), into a larcenous scheme: the pair will rob a suburban mom-and-pop jewelry store that appears to be the quintessential easy target. The problem is, the store owners are Andy and Hank's real mom and pop, and when the seemingly perfect crime goes awry, the damage sends them hurtling toward a shattering clash that may obliterate their already precarious lives.

programme.title: The Ron Clark Story
programme.desc: A small-town teacher relocates to one of the toughest classrooms in the country.

programme.title: Killing Them Softly
programme.desc: Jackie Cogan, an enforcer, is hired to restore order after three idiots rob a mob-protected poker game.

programme.title: Traffik
programme.desc: A getaway turns to terror after Brea and John accidentally discover the hidden world of a brutal biker gang.

programme.title: Vendetta
programme.desc: When his wife is killed, a detective goes to prison to seek vengeance.

programme.title: Blood Out
programme.desc: Michael goes undercover to take justice into his own hands after his brother is murdered by a gang.

programme.title: Joe Baby
programme.desc: A money collector for a private investigator is hired to track down 10 million dollars stolen in a dodgy real estate deal, opening a complex can of worms.

programme.title: Andromeda: 1883
programme.desc: Space marine Casey Reynolds travels into the past to stop a killer android bent on wiping out the one person crucial to saving the human race.

programme.title: 12 To Midnight
programme.desc: A detective gets kicked off the force when the murder of his wife drives him to drink. When a new string of murders seem connected to her death, he soon finds himself squaring off against a killer who appears to be superhuman.

programme.title: Karma: Death at Latigo Springs
programme.desc: Charley Stevens moves to the town where her recently deceased mother grew up; after arriving, she is plagued with strange nightmares that tease a horrific event, hinting at why her mother left town.

programme.title: Lone Star Shark 2
programme.desc: A new victim has been chosen to face off against a ravenous shark in a Texas lake.

programme.title: Master Z: Ip Man Legacy
programme.desc: Defeated by Master Ip, martial arts expert Cheung Tin Chi tries to lead a normal life in Hong Kong until triad leaders draw him back into fighting.

programme.title: Train to Busan
programme.desc: A man (Gong Yoo), his estranged daughter and other passengers become trapped on a speeding train during a zombie outbreak in South Korea.

programme.title: Kung Fu Stuntmen
programme.desc: Filmmakers showcase the dedication and struggles of kung fu stuntmen and action directors working in Hong Kong cinema during the 1960s-'80s.

programme.title: Max Cloud
programme.desc: When a teenage gamer accidentally opens a portal into her favorite side-scroller, she becomes trapped in a notorious intergalactic prison, home to the galaxy's most dangerous villains. To escape, she must finish the game with a little help from her not-so-savvy friend on the outside -- or remain a 16-bit character forever.

programme.title: Enter the Fat Dragon
programme.desc: A police officer investigates the mysterious death of a suspect who was in his custody during a routine transfer.

programme.title: Nightmare PTA Moms
programme.desc: Gail joins the PTA, only to find it run by power-hungry mom Vanessa.

programme.title: Beyond the Headlines: The Tiffany Rubin Story
programme.desc: It was every parent's nightmare.

programme.title: Nightmare Neighborhood Moms
programme.desc: Charlotte and her daughter Jordan want to start a neighborhood watch.

programme.title: Nightmare Pageant Moms
programme.desc: Emilia, Sophie enter Mother-Daughter pageant for scholarship in crisis.

programme.title: Nightmare PTA Moms
programme.desc: Gail joins the PTA, only to find it run by power-hungry mom Vanessa.

programme.title: The Soulmate Search
programme.desc: Lindsay and Will bond after agreeing the dating service that matched them has made a mistake.

programme.title: New Dogs, Old Tricks
programme.desc: Alan handles Women’s Lit as Tiny’s Place plan is approved.

programme.title: A Royal Recipe for Love
programme.desc: When Emma is hired to bake for a royal cake, she finds herself at odds with a Prince.

programme.title: The Wedding Rule
programme.desc: Gemma and Max, former childhood friends are bequeathed a restaurant but must pretend to be married.

programme.title: Inspiring Love
programme.desc: When Isla’s art unexpectedly sells to Mark, a second commission sees them falling in love.

programme.title: For the Love of Lily
programme.desc: A career-minded city girl must build a garden with a plant-loving country boy to appease her boss.

programme.title: The Climb
programme.desc: The breath-taking story of two men ... one with a courageous heart who seeks God; the other with an independent spirit who relies on his own strength.

programme.title: CODE
programme.desc: Dallas artist MALIK SCHAEFER, also known as CODE, is a rising star in the underground rap scene. Although his checkered past has led him down the wrong road at times, with the help of his best friend, STYLES, Malik is back on the fast-track to success. That is, until he reconnects with the dangerous love of his life, SOPHIA ORTEGA, who threatens to destroy it all. Malik must decide to either follow his heart or live by the code that has served him well for many years: family over everything.

programme.title: Falcon Rising
programme.desc: Chapman is an ex-marine in Brazil's slums, battling the yakuza outfit who attacked his sister and left her for dead.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: The New Girl
programme.desc: When her sister Monica suddenly disappears under suspicious circumstances, Malorie becomes convinced that Monica's seemingly perfect husband is hiding something sinister. She then launches her own investigation and unearths a twisted web of lies, abuse, and even political conspiracy. Malorie must confront dangerous secrets that not only threaten her sister's life but her own.

programme.title: Suga Babies
programme.desc: Follow a gang of femme fatales in the dangerous world of drugs and money in this urban tale of power and wealth. They all want Suga, but it ain't sweet.

programme.title: Street Crimes Unit
programme.desc: Detective Mills and Detective Smith are assigned to the Street Crimes Unit of the Police Department. While on a stakeout one day, Mills and Smith see a local politician, Alex Johnstone, arguing with his mistress. Days later the mistress goes missing. Mills decides to investigate Alex Johnstone, even after being told by his supervisor not to do so. Mills convinces Smith that they must find the evidence that connects Alex Johnstone to the missing woman before he tracks them down.

programme.title: Priceless: The Dawn
programme.desc: Having platinum records and superstar rosters seem like easy work to music industry legends Donica Blair and Shequell Harris, but when they want to start their own label, they become targets in more ways than they are prepared for. Adding Seven to the mix in an already uneven playing field leads to unorthodox measures to protect Don and save her life. Quell, on the other hand, may have gotten exactly what she asked for when she calls Silk to help them out.

programme.title: Priceless: The Score
programme.desc: After starting their new record label, Donica Blair and Shequell Harris have entered a dangerous arena full of guns, lies, and deceit. Now they are racing against time to right their wrongs and save their own lives before they are next in a string of murders. Meanwhile their partner Seven, has fallen victim to the deadliest of them all; the Black widow.

programme.title: How Did We Get Here
programme.desc: Four struggling couples attend a wellness retreat led by Dr. Sellers. Melonie and Derrick struggle with issues of infidelity, Omar and Sonya struggle with commitment, Chastity and Monae have differing opinions on parenthood, and Stephanie and Delvin are struggling with their intimacy. As the couples dive into their treatment, they must confront their own demons to move forward. Will they make it out together? Or go their separate ways?
The 13th year of the new millenium brings a 13th month, and with it unlocks the gates from hell.

programme.title: Bound
programme.desc: After a younger man introduces her to BDSM, a wealthy real estate brokers’ life starts a deadly spiral.

programme.title: Megalodon: The Frenzy
programme.desc: A “frenzy” of five Megalodons - spawn of the original - terrorizes the open ocean.

programme.title: Planet of the Sharks
programme.desc: In the future, sharks dominate the planet, led by a mutated alpha shark, as human survivors live in terror.

programme.title: The Twisters
programme.desc: Dozens of deadly tornadoes converge, merging into a mega twister set to obliterate the United States.

programme.title: Two Evil Eyes
programme.desc: In George Romero's The Facts In The Case Of Mr. Valdemar, a conniving wife and her lover use a hypnotic trance to embezzle a fortune from her dying husband, only to receive some chilling surprises from beyond the grave. Then in Dario Argento's The Black Cat, a deranged crime scene photographer is driven to brutal acts of madness and murder by his girlfriend's new pet.

programme.title: Tales From The Void
programme.desc: When a mysterious black square appears out of thin air, mistrust grows between neighbors until conspiracies overtake reason.

programme.title: Tales From The Void
programme.desc: Juan and his friends like to spend their evenings pranking the neighborhood. But what happens when they hit the wrong kind of house?

programme.title: Nomads (Edited Version)
programme.desc: A French anthropologist intends to settle peacefully in Los Angeles, but a mystical band of deadly evil spirits(lead by Adam Ant)-- the incarnation of nomads he once studied -- materialize to haunt him.

programme.title: The Editor (Edited Version)
programme.desc: Rey Ciso was once the greatest editor the world had ever seen. Since a horrific accident left him with four wooden fingers on his right hand, he's had to resort to cutting pulp films and trash pictures. When the lead actors from the film he's been editing turn up murdered at the studio, Rey is fingered as the number one suspect. The bodies continue to pile up in this absurdist giallo-thriller as Rey struggles to prove his innocence and learn the sinister truth lurking behind the scenes.

programme.title: Beast from Haunted Cave
programme.desc: A giant spider stalks a band of thieves in this schlocky thriller from Gene Corman Productions. The story follows gangster Alexander Ward and his crew as they enlist the help of a local ski-instructor in the heist of several gold bars. But when they use explosives to create a distraction, they disturb the lair of a massive spider! Monte Hellman, the man behind such cult favorites as Two-Lane Blacktop, made his directorial debut with Beast From Haunted Cave. Trivia note: This movie debuted on a double bill with The Wasp Woman.

programme.title: The Room
programme.desc: Kate and Matt find a hidden room in their new home that has the power to materialize anything they want. But some wishes can have dire consequences...

programme.title: Satan's Slaves
programme.desc: Satan's Slaves

programme.title: Satan's Slaves: Communion
programme.desc: After moving from their home to an apartment building, a new terror awaits.

programme.title: Son
programme.desc: When a young boy falls ill to a mysterious illness, his mother must decide how far she will go to protect him from terrifying forces in her past.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Garden Party" - Mrs. Drysdale has a garden party and 'invites' the Clampetts by mistake. They end up back by their 'cement pond' to handle the 'overflow' from Mrs. Drysdale's party. A gun shot by Granny starts the real party.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Turkey Day" - Elly bonds with the Thanksgiving turkey so the family can't bring themselves to kill it; Mrs Drysdale hires some Indians to take a Thanksgiving picture, which puts Granny on the warpath.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Go Hollywood" - When Jake Clampett tells Jed he's his cousin, he's welcomed into the home and soon has everyone but Jed thinking about and dressing for a role in their next movie.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Are Overdrawn" - The Clampetts receive an overdraft from the bank intended for the deadbeat actor J.D. 'Jake' Clampett, while he gets their statement and starts to spend their money.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Chickadee Returns" - Jethro thinks he's in love with Chickadee Laverne, a stripper he met at the bank. She and the Clampetts have meet and have different ideas about what an "engagement" is, while Miss Hathaway tries to avert this disaster.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jethro's First Love" - Jethro decides that he wants to go a-courtin', so after Uncle Jed gives him a little education, he finds himself a woman--who happens to be a stripper. As usual, neither she or the Clampetts actually understand what the other is really saying.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampette Look" - Cynthia Fenwick makes her filthy rich mother dress in the "Clampett Look" when they visit the Clampetts, since she thinks Elly is a fashion maven. That only reinforces the Clampett's belief that the Fenwicks are poor.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly Starts to School" - Mrs. Drysdale recommends Elly for a high-brow finishing school in hopes that humiliating her will drive the Clampetts away; but instead Miss Hathaway convinces the girls at the school that Elly is a fashion trend-setter. Elly invites the filthy rich Fenwicks over, thinking they're destitute.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Granny's Garden" - Grannie has everyone up at the crack of down to plow the front yard for her garden, while Mr. Drysdale, Miss Hathaway, and a stubborn mule try to stop her.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Hair-Raising Holiday" - After Granny cuts off the top of Drysdale and Clyburn's (his doctor) hair for some homegrown remedy, Clyburn tries to keep her from ever doctoring again. Meanwhile, the Clampetts are looking forward to the Possum Day parade, which of course isn't celebrated in Beverly Hills, so Drysdale has to figure out how to have one to keep them happy.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Gets the Misery" - Jed cheers up Granny by pretending to be sick so she can doctor him. Soon Drysdale and his doctor, Clyburn, are subject to her methods as well.

programme.desc: Ads for this Roy Rogers classic roared HEAD FOR THE MUSICAL HILLS...ROY IS GUNNIN' FOR THRILLS! And thrills are what you'll get when you watch this fun, action packed B-Western from Republic Pictures. There's some great comic relief from Andy Devine and Chester Conklin, two of Hollywood's all-time greats. This old-fashioned shoot-'em-up was directed by William Witney, who would go on to direct many of television's top western sagas, including Bonanza, Wagon Train, and The Wild, Wild West.

programme.title: Cisco Kid
programme.desc: Cisco witnesses a dog identifying the murderer of his master. He then tries to prove that the dog is right. Starring Duncan Renaldo as The Cisco Kid, and Leo Carillo as Pancho. This weekly adventure series was a favorite of the 1950s, following two pals as they traveled the old west, righting wrongs and helping the underdog. The show was produced by ZIV Television, the same company that gave us Highway Patrol and Sea Hunt, and was nominated for a Primetime Emmy in 1951. This episode aired in 1950.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: From time to time you have moments that will change the way think about something. That is what happened for us while hunting the Scimitar Oryx with Republic Outdoors. Hunting is more than harvesting animals, it’s conservation, and in this story, we learn how a species nearly extinct gets another chance to live.

programme.title: Storytellers - "Disabled Outdoorsmen"
programme.desc: Storytellers - "Disabled Outdoorsmen"

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: In 2010, Amberly Snyder’s life changed forever when an accident left her paralyzed from the waist-down. Her comeback story not unlike something you’d see in a movie…in fact it inspired one. Disabled Outdoorsmen USA contacted TTHA and set up this hunt and we were fortunate enough to be able to capture just some of Amberly’s story and her harvest of a beautiful Axis buck at the G2 Ranch in the spring of 2022.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon welcome Angie Rito and Scott Tacinelli, the chef-owners behind the celebrated New York restaurant Don Angie, for a lively episode dedicated to Italian American comfort food. The conversation begins with admiration for the restaurant’s iconic lasagna before shifting to the main event: a deeply flavorful, “life-changing” meatball recipe inspired by a blend of both chefs’ grandmothers’ traditions. As Angie and Scott walk through their process, they share chef-driven techniques like using a milk-soaked panade for moisture, puréed onions for even flavor distribution, and roasted garlic to avoid harsh raw bites. The hosts jump in with questions and personal anecdotes, sparking a broader discussion about Italian versus Italian American cooking, rule-breaking in the kitchen, and regional food identity—from Cleveland to New York. The group also plays a spirited round of “Discuss,” debating topics like garlic usage, cheese on seafood, and proper spaghetti etiquette, leading to humorous and opinionated exchanges. Along the way, they highlight the philosophy behind Don Angie’s food: bold, unapologetic flavors built on cheese, garlic, and tradition—without strict adherence to old-world rules. As the meatballs finish baking, they’re tossed in a simple, bright tomato sauce and served family-style, earning rave reviews from the hosts for their rich texture and layered flavor. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway of the chefs’ cookbook and a final celebration of Italian American cuisine, emphasizing creativity, collaboration, and the joy of cooking without limits.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon take on the ultimate comfort food by each creating their own version of a grilled cheese sandwich, showcasing three distinct styles that range from classic to elevated. Michael leads with a rich and decadent Croque Madame, complete with a homemade mornay sauce, gruyère, ham, and a perfectly cooked egg on top, sharing tips on building flavor and texture through technique. Carla follows with a Southern-inspired twist, hollowing out a brioche bun to stuff it with pimento cheese and tangy chow chow pickles, emphasizing balance and texture in every bite. Clinton rounds things out with a fan-inspired take on a classic grilled cheese, combining American, cheddar, and gruyère cheeses on rye bread, using mayonnaise for an evenly crisp, golden exterior. Throughout the episode, the trio trades stories, jokes, and cooking insights, debating everything from sandwich ratios to restaurant culture. They later shift into a lively “overrated vs. underrated” game, diving into food trends, brunch culture, and even aging, blending humor with candid reflections. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway and appreciation for sponsors, leaving viewers inspired to experiment with their own grilled cheese creations at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon welcome legendary pitmaster Melissa Cookston for a lively, flavor-packed episode centered around Mexican-inspired smoked chicken wings. The conversation kicks off with playful banter about grilling, live-fire cooking, and Melissa’s impressive barbecue credentials, setting the stage for a masterclass in flavor. Melissa walks the hosts through her approach to wings, emphasizing dry rubs over heavy sauces and showcasing a spice blend inspired by her travels to Mexico, featuring garlic, onion, smoked Hungarian paprika, ancho chili powder, cumin, and oregano. Along the way, the group dives into deeper cooking philosophy—trusting your instincts, cooking with your senses, and understanding that great barbecue is about feel as much as technique. Melissa shares insights on smoking temperatures, wood choices, and why over-smoking can ruin a dish, comparing it to over-salting. The energy stays high as Clinton hilariously spirals into wing-induced chaos, while Carla and Michael debate everything from condiments to air fryers. The episode also highlights Melissa’s new cookbook, Fanning the Flames, described as a love letter to her global culinary travels and the people who inspired her. After cooking, the group digs into the wings, followed by a round of “Chew It or Screw It” and fan mail, blending humor with heartfelt moments. The episode wraps with giveaways and a celebration of community, creativity, and the joy of cooking with fire.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this heartfelt Mother’s Day episode of Chewed Up, Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly reflect on the women who shaped them while Carla leads the kitchen with a show-stopping mimosa brioche French toast topped with frangipane. Inspired by her mother’s love of simple breakfast classics like pancakes and French toast, Carla elevates the dish into a baked, brunch-worthy centerpiece featuring brioche, apricot jam, almond-rich frangipane, and a champagne-orange glaze. As the dish comes together, the hosts share nostalgic stories about their childhoods—from weeknight dinners and Sunday sauces to special outings at places like Bob Evans and Chinese restaurants—highlighting how food connects to family memories. The conversation flows with humor and warmth, touching on generational differences in cooking habits and dining culture. Once the dish is baked to golden perfection, the trio digs in, praising its rich texture and balance of flavors, with Carla’s creative twist winning everyone over. The episode continues with playful segments like “What Would Mom Say,” where the hosts share funny and revealing lessons from their mothers, followed by fan mail and a casual food discussion. The show wraps with a giveaway of Carla’s personal artwork and a sincere tribute to moms everywhere, celebrating the love, lessons, and meals that define family traditions.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall shares her ultimate biscuit-making secrets while teaching two different techniques for creating perfect homemade biscuits. Joined by Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly, Carla walks through her trusted biscuit recipe while revealing the small adjustments and baking tricks she has collected over years of cooking, tasting, and learning from other biscuit makers. The conversation centers around how technique can completely transform the same recipe, as Carla demonstrates both drop biscuits baked in muffin tins and laminated layered biscuits made through folding and stacking dough. Along the way, the hosts discuss the importance of cold butter, the role of steam in creating flaky layers, and why full-fat buttermilk makes a major difference in texture and tenderness. Michael and Clinton enthusiastically jump into the science behind baking while keeping the conversation playful, debating butter pockets, crispy edges, and the superiority of muffin-tin stuffing. Carla also shares clever practical tricks, including using oil instead of excess flour on the work surface and flipping biscuit dough for better height and rise. The trio reflects on how cooking competition shows continue to teach even experienced chefs new techniques, reinforcing the episode’s theme that cooking is a constant learning process. After pulling both styles of biscuits from the oven, the hosts compare the textures and flavors of each version while slathering them with butter, jam, and honey. The episode wraps with Carol from Columbus, Ohio being selected as the “Fan of the Day,” earning a personalized message from the hosts before everyone signs off stuffed and satisfied.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon reunite in the kitchen for the very first episode of CHEWED UP. Carla makes Sweet Potato Hummus, Michael debates the name, Clinton avoids cooking, and the chaos kicks off immediately.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon dive into a tailgating-themed episode of CHEWED UP with Michael leading the charge. He fries up crispy Pork Schnitzel Sliders while sharing wild football stories, Carla admits she’s barely tailgated in her life, and Clinton once again finds himself assigned kitchen tasks he may or may not be qualified for. Between talk of The Chew’s abrupt cancellation, psychic predictions, and potato roll “squish” debates, the chaos continues.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton ushers in sweater weather with a decadent, over-the-top hot chocolate while the crew revisits the infamous "Eggnog-gate" story - a stomach-turning behind-the-scenes tale from their network TV days. Cozy vibes meet barely appropriate storytelling.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon kicks off the week with Crispy Baked Chicken Wings tossed in a hot honey and lime glaze. The hosts look back on the freeing final weeks of The Chew before its abrupt end, and Michael delivers a passionate rant against air fryers. The trio introduces their new interactive "Fan Fridge" segment, and Clinton shares a hilarious story about how eating messy wings on a date made him realize he was gay!

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall brings the heat with a comforting Nashville Hot Chicken Mac and Cheese. Carla builds a heavily spiced, vibrant cheese sauce and folds in crispy chicken and large elbow pasta. The hosts reminisce about Carla's famous ground nut stew from Top Chef, discuss their competitive streaks, and debate the merits of retro candies like Peeps and the 100 Grand bar.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly embraces fall with a recipe for Pumpkin Spice Muffins that double as cupcakes when topped with frosting. While the treats bake, the hosts debate whether muffins belong at breakfast or dessert and share their absolute worst live television disasters including Michael cooking during an active fire and Clinton slicing his finger on live TV.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: In the debut episode of Simply Symons, Michael Symon welcomes viewers into his Venice, California front yard alongside his wife Liz Symon and their friend Olivia Annacone Cisic for an unfiltered, laid-back cookout. Celebrating Cinco de Mayo, Michael prepares simple yet flavorful black cod fish tacos on a plancha, walking through his approach to seasoning, cooking fish by sight, and building a bright, crunchy slaw with cabbage, citrus, and charred poblanos. The conversation flows easily as the trio shares cooking tips, debates ingredients like corn versus flour tortillas, and jokes about knife sharpening habits, cilantro preferences, and home cooking realities. Liz complements the meal with a refreshing jalapeño margarita, balancing heat with fresh lime juice, simple syrup, and a hint of orange liqueur, while adding her own creative twist with lemon and bitters. Throughout the episode, the group emphasizes flexibility in cooking—encouraging viewers to adjust flavors, garnishes, and spice levels to their own tastes. Lighthearted banter continues as they assemble tacos, discuss plating ideas, and highlight optional additions like crema and avocado. The episode wraps with a taste test, enthusiastic reactions to both the tacos and cocktail, and a reminder that great food doesn’t have to be complicated—just fresh, simple, and made with joy.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly elevates lunchtime with savory French Onion Soup Tartines, topped with melted Gruyere. While the onions caramelize, the trio discusses their love for French food, debates the perfect early dinner time for getting older, and shares hilarious stories about awkward fan encounters including the time a woman on a crowded train told Clinton he looked "so much fatter in person".

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: "Why You Should Stay Indoors, Reservation Hacks, and So You Wanna… Be an Expediter?" - Dave opens up the show with jobs he would have wanted to do if he was 22 years old, which turns into speculation on the personality traits it might take to be a pyrotechnic engineer. The show continues as Dave provides strong evidence for why everyone should stay indoors and discusses the (likely related) channels he’s watching on YouTube. Dave and Chris then discuss some hacks for how to get a reservation at busy restaurants. Things round out with So You Wanna … Be an Expediter? where Dave and Chris show their appreciation for the unsung heroes of a restaurant.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: "Who Wears Pajamas? Top 5 Asian Cuisines Updated, and Sleeping Quirks" - Dave kicks off the episode with three things about 'Oppenheimer'—including some recommendations to help with understanding the film. Ask Dave addresses a question about where to work in the restaurant industry and how to approach a shift into a culinary career. Dave then launches into a Slice about sleeping habits, with a particular eye on pajamas, and he plays a new game where Dave, Euno, Cory, and Victoria try to guess what celebrities wear to bed (pajamas, basketball shorts, or nothing). The episode continues with a MOIF on all matters of sleeping, including fighting jet lag, showering habits, and the late-night refrigerator degustation. Finally, the episode concludes with Dave's revision to his Top Five Asian Cuisines (with some very surprising exclusions) and a new segment where Dave professes his love for toum.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: "Debate Club - Sandwiches, Being Late to Things and When to Cancel a Restaurant Reservation" - Dave starts the top of the show by talking about his personal journey through being punctual—and what specific type of meeting he’s been late for or missed entirely. The discussion veers into Zoom etiquette and “flexes.” Ask Dave sees Dave tackle a highly problematic practice: booking multiple restaurant reservations for the same time and canceling with minimal notice (or no-showing!). Dave walks through the havoc that a canceled reservation can wreak on a restaurant’s profitability and implores diners to cancel reservations with as much notice as possible. Euno joins the show for a Debate Club With Dave, where the two argue about which sandwich reigns supreme. Tensions pile high in the battle for sandwich supremacy: banh mi (Dave) vs. the muffuletta (Euno). The two also list their favorite sandwich countries—with Dave having a surprising pick for no. 1. Dave caps off the episode with his mixed feelings about tteokbokki’s rapid rise in popularity.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "Salt-Broiled Fish" - After a visit to the studio from Liwei Liao (@dry_aged_fish_guy), Chris finds himself with a beautiful sea bream that’s begging to be prepared simply. Salt-roasting, it is.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "Broccoli Cheddar Twists" - Another day, another attempt by Chris to sneak vegetables into his kids diet. This time, he tries a TikTok recipe for broccoli cheddar twists using frozen puff pastry.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: An enchanting city, Barcelona is brimming with inspirations left by the greatest of artists. But this charming city has another jewel in its crown - La Boqueria food market. Take a journey through the senses with our lively characters and uncover their gastronomic secrets.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: Perhaps one of the most beautiful and well-organized food market buildings in Europe is the Mercado Central of Valencia. Built on the area where an open-air market stood for centuries, the Valencian style Art Noveau construction is a 'Cathederal of senses'.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: The largest indoor market in Budapest is also the most elegant in Europe! Spanning over three levels, the impressive central food market is long recognized as a marvel. Whether it be the freshest fish, the richest goose liver, or the sweetest paprika that you're looking for, you'll find it. But don't forget the basement: this is where you will find the very best pickled cabbage in the country!

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: At the Mercato Centrale in San Lorenzo, one can taste the best food treasures Tuscany has on offer: from the olive oil of the Fiesole hills to the onions of the medieval borough of Certaldo and up to the Natural Reserve of San Marcello with its Tuscan cured meats. Here the simple and genuine tastes are king.

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Set sail with Chef Walter Staib on a two-part adventure down the majestic Danube River! Part 1 charts a course through Germany and Austria, exploring stunning valleys amidst Europe's heart. Discover historic sites, artistic treasures, and culinary delights at every stop along this unforgettable journey.

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Our journey continues through Austria, Slovakia, and ending in the Hungarian city of Budapest. We’ll marvel at architectural wonders and savor the flavors of the region with Chef Walter Staib as he prepares traditional meals aboard a luxurious cruise ship. Don't miss this unforgettable finale on the mighty Danube!

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Journey to St. John's, Newfoundland, to discover the deep connection locals have with nature's bounty, and meet inspiring individuals who fish, farm, and cook with generations of tradition. Savor unique recipes prepared over crackling fire-pits, including the king of St. John's cuisine: cod.

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Join Chef Walter Staib on a journey to Colorado Springs, where we'll explore the history of this scenic landscape, from the indigenous Ute people through the era of homesteaders. At the Historic Rock Ledge Ranch, savor delicious recipes that have withstood the test of time. Don't miss out on this opportunity to experience the flavors and heritage of Colorado Springs!

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Chef Walter Staib visits Curacao to unravel the delicious stories behind this vibrant Caribbean island. We'll meet local chefs who share their culinary passion through mouthwatering dishes like stewed papaya, okra soup, and stuffed Dutch cheese. Unforgettable flavors and island adventures await as we explore Curacao's heritage and unique cuisine, making this an episode you won’t want to miss!

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Chef Walter Staib dives into the coastal bounty of Port Aransas, Texas! Discover legendary fishing tales, explore the vibrant local culture, and savor unique seafood creations with Chef Pete Mims. Experience how Texas spices meet Gulf Coast flavors in a culinary adventure that blends tradition and innovation. Join us for an unforgettable journey through the rich, diverse tastes of coastal Texas.

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Saddle up for Southern charm as Chef Walter Staib rides through Nashville's history at Belle Meade Mansion. Explore the melodic roots of Country Music, visit iconic landmarks, and whip up delicious Southern eats like Country ham, fruitcake, and syllabub. Experience Nashville’s rich cultural heritage and culinary delights in this captivating journey.

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Unveil Charleston's soul as we explore the layers of its history at the International African American Museum and a Lowcountry rice farm. Learn crabbing secrets from a local pro, then cook with a passionate chef, celebrating Southern staples of the local cuisine.

programme.desc: The Today’s Homeowner crew travelled to snowy Eagan, Minn., home of The Family Handyman studio, to test out some of our favorite homeowner hacks with the magazine’s senior editor, Travis Larson, and contributing editor, Spike Carlsen.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Each year on Today’s Homeowner we feature a contest called “Win Danny and His Crew.” The winner gets our help with their list of household chores, and this year that lucky homeowner is Tamera Pippin of Lynchburg, Va. She was excited to welcome our crew to her 1958 home, which was in amazingly good condition for its age.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Homeowners Kenneth and Kym Sigler have lived in their home for 15 years and plan to stay there. So, we helped these homeowners who wanted to improve, rather than move.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: We added a spacious 400-square foot paver patio and a table made of stone for our winner of the 'Win Danny" Contest.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Robert and Leigh Bailey recently bought their home, and there were several areas of the house and yard that needed attention. So, we helped the Baileys tackle some maintenance chores, and we used our 4 Seasons of Homeownership summer checklist to guide our efforts.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Danny adds improvements to his backyard, like adding a dream outdoor kitchen and beautiful outdoor living area.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Karen O’Leary lives in a quiet, little Connecticut town called Guilford, located on the Long Island Sound. While the town boasts beautiful, old houses, historic landmarks and a thriving downtown district, Karen’s home was lacking in character. The house’s exterior was a bland mix of aluminum and vinyl siding that resulted from additions and updates over the years.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Sarah and her cat Bodhi have lived in a van for the past two years in western Canada. She gives us a full tour of her classic camper van. Sarah also shares her personal experience about what Vanlife is like for her and why she chose the van that she did.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Electrician builds out a 4x4 sprinter into a modern camper that they are able to both live in and work mobile. This build is done with aluminum and pvc boards, has solar, a composting toilet and everything they need to live comfortably. They also share insights from life on the road.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Oaxaca, Mexico: Chef Tony Bourdain explores the exotic and colorful indigenous zone of Mexico's Oaxaca region where he tastes toasted grasshoppers and other delicacies. His adventure ends with mariachi music and, of course, lots of tequila.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Join Tony on his quest to characterize the perfect bar in New York City. Discover the importance of a maniacal bartender, the proper chemical makeup of drinks, the joys of seedy dive-bars, and the need for late-night snacks.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Tony sets out to find the real Singapore, with its multicultural restaurants and food markets. He samples prescription food dishes, explores the Geyland Serai Market, and rides the clean and efficient local subway.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: St. Petersburg, Russia: Tony continues his mission with a tour of local working-class specialties. He tries his hand at ice-fishing, drinks a lot of vodka, feasts on Russian foods, eats smoked fish and ends the outing with a dip in the outdoor cold pool.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Cambodia: With a friend along for the ride, Tony travels to Phnom Penh, Cambodia, where he explores a food culture that is both pleasing to the palette and slightly frightening.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: In the Caribbean paradise of St. Martin, Tony eats a healthy Rastafarian meal with a freedom fighter, helps prepare grub for a food caravan, discovers foodstuffs not available at American supermarkets, and finally relaxes at a beach bar.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Scotland: Beginning in Glasgow, Tony experiences the heart-clogging glory of deep fried pizza, deep fried mars bars, and deep fried pickled eggs! Moving on to more traditional fare, Tony tastes haggis and learns to hunt and fish for his food.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: London, England: In London, Tony attempts to dispel the myth that all English food is disgusting and over-boiled. Tony tries Nose to Tail Eating, explores traditional fare at Pie and Mash Shoppes, and then visits a predominantly Indian community.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Rural Japan: Tony decides to look for a more relaxing, traditional Japanese culinary experience in the countryside. Tony travels to an old-style Japanese inn that specializes in kaiseki cuisine, ancient tea ceremony, and local ingredients.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Melbourne, Australia boasts hard-living, good-natured, and eccentric lovers of food - Tony's kind of people. In search of the outlaws of food, Tony explores a pasteurization-free cheese factory, and an old-world cured jamon workshop.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Which style of BBQ reigns supreme - Kansas City, Houston, or North Carolina? Tony journeys to all three cities to taste for himself the secrets behind sauces, wood pits, and slow smokers.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: San Sebastian, Spain: Tony explores the food-crazed Basque culture. Tony's guide, Chef Luis Irizar, takes Tony into the underworld of Basque gastronomic societies. Later, Tony explores the city's tapas bars.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: St. Petersburg, Russia: Tony plays spy in Russia and sets off to locate the best blinis and borscht St. Petersburg has to offer.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Puebla, Mexico: According to Tony, the best line cooks in NYC come from Mexico, so he travels to the Puebla, and enjoys mole sauces, toasted ant eggs, fried worms, and the legendary pulque (a slightly hallucinogenic drink made from cactus sap).

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Portugal & Southwest France: Visiting his boss's family farm, Tony helps slaughter and feast on a pig the family has been fattening all year. Then, Tony heads to France to experience the rich delights of a foie gras farm.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: A spontaneous layover in Bangkok, Thailand leads Tony into the city, where he tries dishes of deep-fried frog skins and pla rah, a fish-paste that has fermented anywhere from three months to a year.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A man arrested for domestic violence threatens to put all the officers in jail. Inmates on kitchen duty get into a fist fight over a dish towel. A woman unsure about her sexual identity is arrested for trespassing at a friend’s home. Officers find an unusual bit of clothing when a man is changed into a jail uniform.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A man arrested on drug warrants is upset about the BBQ brisket he left on the grill. An intoxicated ladies' man flirts with the jail staff and an inmate finds it funny that he sold tree leaves as pot to an undercover officer. Brother turns on brother when a good deed gets mixed with alcohol. A man caught panhandling believes officers should arrest hardened criminals instead of him.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: An eccentric jail regular is welcomed back. Man fleeing Mexico details the horrors he escaped. Young woman describes her lucrative fling as a stripper. A woman finds that trying to drink away a bad boyfriend and then asking the boyfriend for bail money is not a good idea. A man collapses during booking after being arrested for drunk driving. Officers have heart to heart with a depressed inmate going through a breakup with a cheating girlfriend.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A singer in custody performs for the officers. A woman is charged with burglary, and officers see right through a young woman's tears.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Deputies assist arresting officers by removing an angry man from their patrol car. Violent outbursts plague an arrest. One woman struggles with bail. Three drunk men become wildly emotional.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: In Reno, NV a woman becomes violent with officers after having over indulged in boxed wine. In Salt Lake City, UT a drunk man is brought in for urinating in front of a family restaurant and in attempt to expedite his release he requests that the officers "git 'er done". In Reno, NV a former marine is taken in to custody for domestic abuse.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Angry over her arrest, a woman becomes combative with officers. Busted once again for drugs, a smart young woman knows she is making dumb choices but can’t stop using. A man eluding the police swipes food from his workplace before landing in jail. Officers get a laugh from an arrested panhandler who wants to buy a permit for panhandling.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A fight involving female detainees breaks out after one woman accuses the other of staring at her. A man is arrested for shattering the hotel glass door as a result of a fight. An old crooner believes that rap stars are going to bail him out of jail. Handcuffs don’t stop a woman from trying to flirt with an officer.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Officers try to deal with a sloppy and combative drunk. A man gets locked up after he blows all his casino winnings, and a woman gets tricked by her bail bondsman.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A vice unit interrogates several individuals accused of sex crimes. Suicidal threats provoke a high-stakes situation within cell walls. Unable to raise bail, a woman is incarcerated for several days.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A woman is placed into a restraint chair during intake. Punches are thrown and questions are asked after a fight occurs. After dodging a bar tab, a woman is also accused of spiting and biting.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Officers conduct a strip search after an inmate is accused of carrying contraband. A repeat offender dances for deputies and fellow inmates. High on meth, a man acts erratically during booking.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A young father pulls a desperate double robbery to make ends meet. A routine shakedown gets deadly when officers uncover alarming contraband. An inmate plays innocent about her criminal past until confronted by officers. An antsy woman’s brazen behavior tests the patience of officers.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: An intoxicated man tests officers' patience. A woman tries to deceive police with multiple identities. A one-legged biker shows off his balancing skills to officers.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Code Blue alarms sound as inmates start fighting inside the men’s jail. An irate man with a huge beard refuses to remove bands inside his facial hair. A man with deep stab wounds stumbles into jail.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A woman is placed in isolation after disparaging other inmates. Alarms blare while a fight erupts in the shower room among inmates. A man booked into jail is revealed to have a 20-year arrest record.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: In Salt Lake City, UT a belligerent woman is restrained. In Portland, OR a repeat offender is placed in isolation for unruly behavior. In Las Vegas, NV a man opens up about how his friends and family feel about him in a holding cell. In Spokane, WA a man is jailed in an attempt to steal coffee for his friends.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: An intoxicated woman clashes with officers but later shows that good behavior pays off. An experienced prostitute gives solid advice to a younger woman and begs her to get out of the “game.” An angry woman smashes her cheating boyfriend’s windshield.

programme.title: The Wonders of Bali
programme.desc: Bring the exotic sights and sounds of Bali's island paradise into your home with the sounds of nature and beautiful Balinese Music.

programme.title: Beautiful Costa Rica

programme.title: Yosemite Magic
programme.desc: Yosemite is internationally recognized for its granite cliffs, waterfalls, clear streams, giant sequoia groves, lakes, mountains, glaciers, and biological diversity.

programme.title: Scenes from a Creek
programme.desc: Explore the roaring rapids and serene stillness of a lively creek. Observe rushing waters covering rocky dams as they continue down this most picturesque, meandering waterway.

programme.title: Summer Sunset Into Water
programme.desc: Unwind with a beautiful and picturesque New Jersey Shore sunset. Relax as the clouds and birds slowly drift across the sky and the magnificent sun lights the sky on fire.

programme.title: Finny The Shark - Down In The Bay and more
programme.desc: Did you ever see a shark glowing in the dark? Did you ever see a stingray dancing at the ballet? Finny and Mama Shark are singing a rhyming song about all of their silly friends down in the bay! Sing along!

programme.title: Over The Deep Blue Sea - Puppets - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Yo-ho-ho! Review different types of weather while climbing aboard a pirate ship with the Super Simple Puppets! We’re going this way, that way. Forwards, backwards. Up and down, up and down. Over the deep blue sea.

programme.title: Five Little Chicks - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Five Little Chicks is great for nap time and for practicing counting down from five. Try singing along when it’s time for a nap.

programme.title: Rosey & The Rowdy Rustlers Tour Bus - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Rosey and The Rowdy Rustlers are coming to town to kick off their concert tour! But while driving out on the open road, a giant stray tumbleweed gets in the path of their tour bus, making a mighty mess! Rosey, Roxie, and Rude Jenny will have to take a detour to Carl’s Car Wash! With a quick mess assessment, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Rosey and The Rowdy Rustlers’ vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! The band leaves as happy customers, and just in time for the concert!

programme.title: Who Took The Cookie? (Finny the Shark) - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny’s friends are playing a game of Who Took The Cookie From The Cookie Jar. Who do you think took the cookie? The seahorse, the starfish, the octopus, the walrus, the dolphin, or the crab? Let’s play and find out!

programme.title: Moolissa The Underwater Cow - Finny The Shark and more
programme.desc: Finny’s class finds out they have a new special friend coming for a visit, a cow named Moolisa! Being from land, it can be tricky for Moolisa to do everything that Finny’s friends do in the sea, but Moolisa will try her best to share in all the experiences of life in the ocean.

programme.title: Five Little Monsters Jumping On The Bed - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s a silly and slightly spooky version of the classic kids’ rhyme, Five Little Monkeys, with monsters instead of monkeys. It’s a great song for practicing counting down from five, and for teaching little monsters (and children!) not to jump on the bed.

programme.title: Red Light Green Light - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Tobee learned all about traffic safety in school today! He learned how to walk in a crosswalk, to look both ways before crossing a street, and what the colors of a traffic light mean. That reminds Caitie of a song about stopping and going called “Red Light, Green Light.” Together, they sing and dance, stopping and starting again and again. Tobee and Caitie have a blast, and you can join the fun, too! Green light!

programme.title: Twinkle Twinkle Little Star - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: “Twinkle, twinkle little star. How I wonder what you are?” Lulu the Owl and Juno the Star become fast friends in this Super Simple version of one of the world’s most popular nursery rhymes.

programme.title: Weepy French Onion Soup - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Today, the Bumble Nums are going to make Weepy French Onion Soup. But the four weepy onions they need are VERY tricky to find. Will Humble, Grumble, and Stumble be able to spot all the onions and get back to the kitchen without crying too much? Watch and see!

programme.title: Put On Your Shoes - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s a Super Simple Song all about getting dressed! Sing along as we put on our shoes, jacket, hat, and scarf and then head out for a walk with the family.

programme.title: With My Heart - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Feel the warmth and learn the five senses in this heart warming Super Simple sensory song for kids. It’s also a perfect song to celebrate all parents. I can hear with my ears. I can see with my eyes.

programme.title: Let's Count To 100 - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: We are learning to count to 100 with Finny the Shark! This is a perfect numbers song for young kids and preschoolers! Let’s count to 100. Counting’s so much fun. Let’s count to 100. We’ll start from number 1.

programme.title: Seven Steps - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Counting to seven has never been more memorable and fun! Learn numbers with this simple counting song.

programme.title: Goodbye A, Goodbye Z - Super Simple ABCs and more
programme.desc: We're saying, "Goodbye" to all of our friends in Alphabet Village. As the school day ends, take a walk through town, and bid farewell to all of the letters.

programme.title: The Bumble Nums - Super Simple Collection 1
programme.desc: Once Humble, Grumble, and Stumble make it to their kitchen with ingredient intact, it's time to make the dish.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Dizzy jet to Jeju, helping Taehoon’s robot save Grandma’s shellfish from a giant fish, then team up to free it. On Mars, Jett and Marty chase Roy's lost bags, while Jinga dazzles with an out-of-this-world tour.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Dizzy join Hazel and Runi in Australia to help animals escape wildfires, then fly to Seoul where tiny Antny enters the Strongest Bug Contest, faces rampaging rivals, and, with Super Wings’ upgrades, helps save the city.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Marty help wash Maxy the giant plane and calm geysers at Oyunuma Pond, then deliver a Wright Flyer to Braden, sparking a time-hop to the Wright brothers, where teamwork lifts spirits and the Flyer takes flight.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett, Lucie, and Runi’s friendly contest becomes teamwork when they rescue toys from a Vietnam factory fire. Soon, Jett races to Dino World, freeing stranded friends and reuniting a T-Rex family as quakes rumble. Super Wings deliver!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Noah and the Dino Wings turn hail into fruit seeds, planting a thriving farm with Jett’s help and saving Dino World from a flood. Later, teamwork and a daring rescue inside Supersaurus bring the friends closer as everyone returns home safe.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: In Sicily, Jett and Curie foil invisibility pranks and stop a wild volcano eruption with Curie’s snow launcher, teaching Renata as chaos turns to learning. Aboard Ned’s ship, Super Electric Suits help them rescue everyone from a junk robot.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: While the World Spaceport celebrates Jimbo’s retirement, Jett faces a UFO and Space Monster, piloting World Robot as chaos erupts. The team unites, time freezes, and Jimbo reveals Megamon is an old friend, choosing to stay with everyone.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and the Super Wings liven up Xiang Xiang’s Tanghulu shop, rescuing everyone from a syrupy mess and drawing big crowds. In Dino World, the Dino Wings halt a Dinobase takeover and reunite a wandering pterosaur hatchling with its mom.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Floods and thunder strike Dino World but Trico overcomes his fear to help Jett and the Dino Wings rescue every dino with paper boats and planes. Later Noah and Mom’s dino costume adventure leads to a mix-up with real maiasauras, and families reunite.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: In Paris, Jett and Curie shrink to save moles from a burst pipe during a wild croquet chase. Later, Jett and Sara help Princess Maribel when magical dress chaos erupts, restoring order so the party sparkles brighter than ever.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: On the moon, Jett and Curie outsmart dust storms and shield a spacecraft to help Kajsa ride her new bike. In Mexico’s Baja Desert, Jett and Marty clear boulders so Big Wheel can race again and roll to victory, powered by Super Wings spirit.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: In Munich, Jett and Marty foil Golden Boy and Girl’s wolf costume prank during Mila’s parade, saving everyone from a river dunk. Later in Singapore, Jett, Traver, and Lien’s robot dogs safely stop runaway giant fireworks, rescuing the festival.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: In Copenhagen, dinos escape through portals as Jett, Lucie, and Super Wings rescue a brachiosaur and guide everyone home. Across the globe, megabot Super Wings unite to send dinos back through swirling portals, saving the world together.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: At a deep-sea base, Super Wings and ocean pals unite to hoist a damaged base after a belt tug-of-war. In Madagascar, Eddie uses comic-book courage to find an elephant bird feather and save his dad in a thrilling cliffside rescue.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Traver inspire Xinyan with Kung Fu mastery, turning chopsticks into her winning move and calming a tiger. In snowy Norway, Jett and Sara stop runaway snowballs so Princess Hedda’s sleigh race ends in victory and laughter.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A wedding spins into chaos as a giant ring escapes, but Big Wing’s catch saves the day and unveils a joyful bride. Later, Jett and Golden Boy use Cross Combo power to tame a hungry dino plant and rescue Golden Girl and Runi.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Babies love animals, and now you can bring animals from the seven continents home to your baby. Meet dozens of exotic animals including Asian pandas, Australian kangaroos and Arctic snow monkeys...all filmed in their beautiful, natural habitats. Rhythms and songs inspired by faraway lands have been orchestrated especially for little ears and encourage your baby to sing, dance and play to the world beats. You'll laugh together at the funny puppet shows featuring moms and their baby animals.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies grow and change, so does the landscape around them. From fiery red leaves to falling snowflakes; from flowers in bloom to fireworks in July, every season of the year has its own special magic just waiting to be discovered.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: World Music introduces you and your little one to beautiful sounds from every continent, bringing a fresh, new experience of music and culture. Sing, dance and play to exotic rhythms that open the door to an appreciation of different cultures from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Learn about the numbers 1,2,3,4 & 5 through rhyme, real-life footage, counting, stories, and repetition - accompanied by classical music and real world imagery.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones become increasingly mobile, their fascination with all things that move intensifies too. On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring exposes babies to the different ways people travel -- on land, by sea, and in the air -- while it presents them with captivating real-world images, funny puppet shows, and beautiful classical music by Beethoven, Haydn, Mozart and Rossini.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Get moving with Baby Einstein! Real, live-action footage and classical melodies engage young viewers as they discover simple words related to movement. Enjoy encouraging melodies and live-action footage of babies and animals as you learn and move alongside your favorite Baby Einstein friends. Dance along as you watch real, live action footage of animals, children, and toys.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As each day passes, babies' awareness of the world around them -- and all the wonders it holds -- continues to develop. Wild animals are especially intriguing and World Animals captures the spirtit of little ones' fascination with wild creatures as it introduces them to animals living in the jungle, in the ocean, and on the savannah.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Learn all about music and rhythm in the world around us! Discover classical music and learn instrument names through narration and repetition. Inspire sweet dreams with gentle melodies and a comforting bedtime story. Learn about classical music from Beethoven, Bach, and Mozart. Sing and dance along to classic rhythmic songs like The Wheels on the Bus and see real-life images of instruments.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Set off on a fun-filled "field trip" with your baby to discover animals found at the park, on the farm, and in your own backyard.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover instruments through narration and repetition. Real-life images and sounds will engage viewers as they dance to upbeat classical favorites from Beethoven, Mozart and more. Join your favorite Baby Einstein characters to discover what makes each instrument unique!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: Cal's Sound Yard
programme.desc: Cal's Sound Yard is a new animated series from Baby Einstein that introduces the letters of the alphabet and the sounds they make. Why all the tracing and repetition? And why are the letters out of order? Don't worry! There's a reason for all that; the content is based on "The Einstein Way" - a curiosity-driven learning philosophy, from industry experts, inspired by Montessori and STEAM.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: From the day their eyes open, babies begin to observe new things around the world and about themselves. Baby Da Vinci From Head to Toe presents them with a playful and interactive introduction to the human body in three languages -- English, Spanish, and French. With a whimsical blend of adorable puppets, real children and animals and famous works of art, this captivating program also features upbeat classical music.

programme.title: Little Red Car - Your Choice Songs
programme.desc: These catchy little songs and rhymes by Little Red Car are fun and entertaining for everyone!

programme.title: Loco Nuts - Funny Cartoon Show for Kids

programme.title: Super Supremes Dinosaur Song & More Kids Songs

programme.title: Dinobees Funny Cartoon Show for Kids

programme.title: Transformex - Futuristic Cartoon Vehicles for Children
